summary: Add the smallest explicit detection state, direct chase movement, visible state feedback, and deterministic verification to the existing idle enemy.
assumptions: The baseline fixture remains the implementation starting point.
The existing Player and Enemy CharacterBody2D nodes remain in main.tscn.
Direct movement is sufficient because the sample scene has no chase obstacles.
The prepared 220-pixel radius and 120-pixel-per-second chase speed require no further product decision.

steps.description: Extend scripts/enemy.gd with a 220-pixel radius, a deterministic is_player_detected() query, and 120-pixel-per-second direct movement that stops outside the radius.
Connect the existing Player to the Enemy in main.tscn and make the enemy label visibly report IDLE or CHASING without external assets.
Extend the existing headless verifier with deterministic outside-radius, inside-radius, chase-distance, and player-input checks and the prepared success token.
Run the complete automated checks, launch the game, and collect the user's visible confirmation before closeout.

excluded: Navigation, obstacle avoidance, or general enemy AI abstractions
Changes outside main.tscn, scripts/enemy.gd, and scripts/verify_fixture.gd
Dashboard, Codex runtime, replay, and quest-completion UI
